breaks: REQ-016 says, "One shared emitter on a single static event taxonomy is the sole writer," but its taxonomy contains no watchdog/fail-closed event; invoking the ordinary REQ-016 channel without adding that event leaves delivery undefined.

The Reliability NFR independently says, "The monitoring framework — heartbeats, invariant pagers, operator paging — is out of v1"; calling the watchdog an exception cannot authorize an actual pager or operator-paging integration.
tensions.amended_resolution: Keep the named REQ-009 safety interlock: it detects stale provider usage or an unconfirmed key flip and cuts the keys immediately. Build no paging integration; instead, atomically emit a static REQ-016 watchdog-failed-closed event to the platform admin by email and in-app notification. Clarify that the excluded monitoring framework is general heartbeat/invariant-pager infrastructure, while the error-spike alert, reconciliation notifications, and this specific fail-closed notification are enumerated exceptions. No founder ruling is needed because the PRD already selects both fail-closed enforcement and REQ-016-style admin notification; only terminology and event registration conflict.
tensions.minimal_prd_change: Make three edits: in REQ-009 replace "the admin is paged" with "a watchdog-failed-closed event notifies the platform admin through REQ-016; no paging integration is used"; in REQ-030 change the monitoring sentence to exclude a "general monitoring framework" while naming the REQ-009 watchdog as a permitted safety interlock; and add "gateway watchdog failed closed → platform admin, email + in-app" to the REQ-016 static taxonomy.

breaks: Candidate (a) is too state-literal. REQ-027 says, "The project re-opens with concierge rematch priority" and that remaining fuel stays on the project, while REQ-009 says, "on abandonment the workspace persists so the successor volunteer inherits it." Restricting availability to status `in_progress` would unexpectedly remove the assistant during a funded post-kickoff rematch and leave its framing undefined.

REQ-006 says, "Fuel is fundable from `draft` onward," while REQ-033 begins, "After funding, the Discovery chat reframes as a read-only project assistant." Because funded pre-kickoff Discovery explicitly exists, the activation trigger must be first kickoff/first entry into `in_progress`, not funding generally; candidate (a)'s proposed one-line change does not repair this sentence.

Candidate (b) contradicts REQ-033's "It is fuel-metered with no free credits," zero-fuel top-up behavior, and "v1 is on-demand text Q&A." REQ-012 also releases leftover fuel, terminates all project keys, and archives the workspace at completion, while Story 6 says, "ai4good does not surface or fund follow-ups in v1; new paid work is a new project." A permanently declining bot is therefore neither an available assistant nor an existing funded surface.
tensions.amended_resolution: Rank (a) over (b), amended as a temporal lifecycle rule: the assistant activates at first kickoff and remains available throughout every subsequent nonterminal state, including abandonment/rematch or revision loops, until the project enters `completed` or `cancelled`; at either terminal transition the bot interface ends, while the completed project's existing static task and activity surfaces remain. Candidate (b)'s strongest argument is preserving the familiar surface and conversation history, but history retention does not require a permanently unusable bot, and the PRD already provides static completed-project context while routing new paid work to a new project. No founder ruling is needed because the terminal fuel release, workspace archival, and new-project rule already determine the only coherent billable-assistant boundary.
tensions.minimal_prd_change: In REQ-033 replace "After funding" with "At the project's first kickoff (first entry into `in_progress`)" and replace "available on the project page from `in_progress` onward" with "available from the project's first entry into `in_progress` until it enters `completed` or `cancelled`, including any intervening post-kickoff nonterminal state."

overall: Both tensions are editorial harmonizations, but both proposals need amendment: T4 must register the watchdog in REQ-016 rather than merely rename paging, and T5 must use a first-kickoff-to-terminal interval rather than the narrower current-state-only form of candidate (a).
